displayName: Mars — Coprates Chasma canyon wall (Valles Marineris)
sourceProduct: HiRISE stereo DTM DTEEC_046764_1660_050812_1660_A01 (East Coprates Chasma wall), University of Arizona / NASA PDS
notes: Swath-aligned 3.6x19.5 km strip down the south wall of eastern Coprates Chasma: 4.70 km of continuous rim-to-floor relief, taller than any cliff on Earth. The grid is rotated ~5 degrees west of north to follow the HiRISE swath (oblique-mercator alignment), so grid-up is along-track, not exactly north; cells are anisotropic (~1.76 m across-track, ~9.52 m along-track). 99.8% real measured pixels; 0.2% were small interior stereo-matching pinholes filled by interpolation. Elevations are metres relative to the Mars areoid.
